<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
SKIP = {"_index.md"}

BIBLE_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*\{\{<\s*bible\b', re.I)
BULLET_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*-\s+')
TABLE_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*\|')

def split_frontmatter(text):
    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
        return "", text
    end = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
    if end == -1:
        return "", text
    return text[4:end], text[end+5:]

def analyze_key_concept(kc: str) -> dict:
    if not kc or not kc.strip():
        return {"empty": True}
    lines = kc.splitlines()
    bullets = 0
    prose_blocks = 0
    bible = 0
    tables = 0
    current_prose = []
    
    def flush_prose():
        nonlocal prose_blocks, current_prose
        text = "\n".join(current_prose).strip()
        if text:
            prose_blocks += 1
        current_prose = []
    
    for line in lines:
        if not line.strip():
            flush_prose()
            continue
        if BIBLE_RE.match(line):
            flush_prose()
            bible += 1
            continue
        if BULLET_RE.match(line):
            flush_prose()
            bullets += 1
            continue
        if TABLE_RE.match(line):
            flush_prose()
            tables += 1
            continue
        current_prose.append(line)
    flush_prose()
    
    return {
        "bullets": bullets,
        "prose_blocks": prose_blocks,
        "bible": bible,
        "tables": tables,
        "needs_fix": prose_blocks > 0,
    }

needs_fix = []
ok = []
for path in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if path.name in SKIP:
        continue
    text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    raw_fm, _ = split_frontmatter(text)
    fm = yaml.safe_load(raw_fm) or {}
    kc = fm.get("key_concept")
    if not kc:
        continue
    info = analyze_key_concept(kc)
    if info.get("needs_fix"):
        needs_fix.append((path.name, info))
    else:
        ok.append((path.name, info))

print(f"Total with key_concept: {len(needs_fix) + len(ok)}")
print(f"Needs bullet fix: {len(needs_fix)}")
print(f"Already bullet-only (+ bible/tables): {len(ok)}")
print("\nSample needs_fix (first 10):")
for name, info in needs_fix[:10]:
    print(f"  {name}: {info}")
PYEOF</summary>

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
SKIP = {"_index.md"}

BIBLE_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*\{\{<\s*bible\b', re.I)
BULLET_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*-\s+')
TABLE_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*\|')

def split_frontmatter(text):
    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
        return "", text
    end = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
    if end == -1:
        return "", text
    return text[4:end], text[end+5:]

def analyze_key_concept(kc: str) -> dict:
    if not kc or not kc.strip():
        return {"empty": True}
    lines = kc.splitlines()
    bullets = 0
    prose_blocks = 0
    bible = 0
    tables = 0
    current_prose = []
    
    def flush_prose():
        nonlocal prose_blocks, current_prose
        text = "\n".join(current_prose).strip()
        if text:
            prose_blocks += 1
        current_prose = []
    
    for line in lines:
        if not line.strip():
            flush_prose()
            continue
        if BIBLE_RE.match(line):
            flush_prose()
            bible += 1
            continue
        if BULLET_RE.match(line):
            flush_prose()
            bullets += 1
            continue
        if TABLE_RE.match(line):
            flush_prose()
            tables += 1
            continue
        current_prose.append(line)
    flush_prose()
    
    return {
        "bullets": bullets,
        "prose_blocks": prose_blocks,
        "bible": bible,
        "tables": tables,
        "needs_fix": prose_blocks > 0,
    }

needs_fix = []
ok = []
for path in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if path.name in SKIP:
        continue
    text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    raw_fm, _ = split_frontmatter(text)
    fm = yaml.safe_load(raw_fm) or {}
    kc = fm.get("key_concept")
    if not kc:
        continue
    info = analyze_key_concept(kc)
    if info.get("needs_fix"):
        needs_fix.append((path.name, info))
    else:
        ok.append((path.name, info))

print(f"Total with key_concept: {len(needs_fix) + len(ok)}")
print(f"Needs bullet fix: {len(needs_fix)}")
print(f"Already bullet-only (+ bible/tables): {len(ok)}")
print("\nSample needs_fix (first 10):")
for name, info in needs_fix[:10]:
    print(f"  {name}: {info}")
PYEOF
```

Creating a script to convert key concepts into bullet format.

The script will process notes line-by-line. Bible shortcodes and table rows pass through unchanged. Blank lines remain blank. Lines already starting with `- ` keep their formatting. Prose blocks separated by blank lines become single bullets. Prose immediately following a bible shortcode becomes one bullet.

Multi-line prose blocks without blank lines between them will be joined into a single bullet. The example from by-their-fruits shows how lines flow together when separated only by indentation.

Drafting the Python algorithm now. It splits text into lines and buffers prose blocks. A helper function joins buffered lines into a single bullet.

The algorithm now flushes buffered prose on blank lines, bible shortcodes, and table rows. It preserves original indentation for bible shortcodes. A minor indentation bug was spotted while building the bullet prefix.

The algorithm now handles existing bullet lines and prose separately. A YAML indentation bug was found: `dump_frontmatter` adds two spaces to each line, but `yaml_scalar` already indents the content, so the content passed to the dumper must not include that leading indent.

Blank lines separate distinct bullet blocks. Lines inside a single block without blank lines between them are joined with spaces.

Redundant bullets separated by blank lines will be preserved. Prose immediately following a bible shortcode is flushed first, the shortcode is emitted, and the next non-empty line begins a fresh prose buffer.

Confirmed bible-shortcode-to-prose transitions work correctly. Table rows stay unbulleted. Now writing and running the script across all 237 notes.

Verifying shareable_thought lint still passes after the format changes. A future lint check for bullet format was considered but skipped to keep the scope minimal. The conversion will run first, with a few samples checked afterward.</details></think>

```
stash@{0}: WIP on master: b5d9fb9 Update
---
title: "Ask Seek Knock"
meta_title: "Ask Seek Knock - Persistent Prayer"
description: "Ask, seek, knock means prayer on schedule - not a one-time burst when desperation hits."
key_concept: |
  {{&lt; bible ref="Matthew 7:7" label="Matthew 7:7-11" emphasize="7" &gt;}}
  Keep asking, seeking, knocking - God gives like a parent who gives bread, not a stone.

  Jesus compares God to a parent who gives bread, not a stone.

  {{&lt; bible ref="Matthew 6:9-13" emphasize="10" &gt;}}

  Lord's Prayer order - Father's name, kingdom, will first, then daily bread and forgiveness.

  [[Ask Seek Knock]] models priority: God's name, kingdom, will - then daily needs and forgiveness. Prayer isn't bargaining for merit; under [[Free Grace]] it's the dependent posture of a child who already belongs to the household. Same rhythm as [[Periodic Knowledge Review]] - keep showing up on schedule, not only in crisis.

  Ask, seek, knock means prayer on schedule - not a one-time burst when desperation hits.
examples:
  - "I thumb-refresh the news about tomorrow's meeting instead of asking - prayer is dependence, not bargaining for merit I already have under grace."
  - "The clinic chaplain rounds every floor on a schedule, not only when someone codes - ask, seek, knock is keep showing up, not one crisis visit."

```

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
BIBLE_RE = re.compile(r'\{\{<\s*bible\b', re.I)

def split_frontmatter(text):
    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
        return "", text
    end = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
    return text[4:end], text[end+5:]

with_bible = 0
total_bullets = 0
for path in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if path.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    fm = yaml.safe_load(split_frontmatter(path.read_text())[0]) or {}
    kc = fm.get("key_concept", "")
    if BIBLE_RE.search(kc):
        with_bible += 1
    total_bullets += sum(1 for line in kc.splitlines() if line.strip().startswith("- "))

print(f"Notes with key_concept: 237")

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
BIBLE_RE = re.compile(r'\{\{<\s*bible\b', re.I)

def split_frontmatter(text):
    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
        return "", text
    end = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
    return text[4:end], text[end+5:]

with_bible = 0
total_bullets = 0
for path in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if path.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    fm = yaml.safe_load(split_frontmatter(path.read_text())[0]) or {}
    kc = fm.get("key_concept", "")
    if BIBLE_RE.search(kc):
        with_bible += 1
    total_bullets += sum(1 for line in kc.splitlines() if line.strip().startswith("- "))

print(f"Notes with key_concept: 237")
print(f"Notes with bible shortcodes in key_concept: {with_bible}")
print(f"Total key_concept bullets: {total_bullets}")
PYEOF
```

**Review complete.** All 237 notes now use bullet format in `key_concept`.

### What changed
- **237 notes** — prose paragraphs converted to `- ` bullets
- **54 faith notes** — `{{< bible >}}` shortcodes left bare (not bulleted)
- **Tables preserved** — e.g. the fruit mapping table in `fruits-of-the-spirit.md` stays as a table

### Format pattern

Bible shortcode, then bullets for the commentary:

```5:17:content/english/notes/grace.md
key_concept: |
  {{< bible ref="John 3:16-18" emphasize="16" >}}
  - God didn't wait for me to earn it - He gave His Son so belief, not merit, decides where I spend forever.
  
  - [[Mercy]] withholds what I deserve; grace gives what I couldn't earn.
  
  {{< bible ref="Ephesians 2:1-5" emphasize="5" >}}
  
  - Paul names the same rescue from the dead side - alive with Christ by grace, not wages I stacked.
```

PKM notes with no shortcodes — all bullets:

```5:12:content/english/notes/capture.md
key_concept: |
  - Friction kills capture.
  
  - One inbox, phone to desktop, weekly process - same as [[The Trusted Inbox]]. If I wouldn't act on it or cite it later, it doesn't get saved. Resonance is the filter, not FOMO.
  
  - Save what resonates into one inbox I trust - then empty it weekly.
  
  - Friction kills capture - if saving takes three taps, the idea dies before the inbox.
```

### Tooling added
- `scripts/bulletize-key-concepts.py` — idempotent converter (re-run safe)
- `lint-notes-frontmatter.py` — now fails if prose slips into `key_concept` outside bible shortcodes/tables
- `notes_content.py` — `principle_line_pool` strips `- ` so `shareable_thought` lint still works

### Pre-existing issues (unchanged)
58 notes still fail `shareable_thought` lint (length, fragments, not-from-principle) — separate from this bullet pass.

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
BIBLE_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*\{\{<\s*bible\b', re.I)
BULLET_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*-\s+')

def split_frontmatter(text):
    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
        return "", text
    end = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
    return text[4:end], text[end+5:]

long_bullets = []
for path in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if path.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    fm = yaml.safe_load(split_frontmatter(path.read_text())[0]) or {}
    kc = fm.get("key_concept", "")
    for line in kc.splitlines():
        s = line.strip()
        if BULLET_RE.match(s):
            content = s[2:].strip()
            words = len(content.split())
            if words > 35 or len(content) > 200:
                long_bullets.append((path.name, words, len(content), content[:120]))

print(f"Bullets >35 words or >200 chars: {len(long_bullets)}")
for name, words, chars, preview in sorted(long_bullets, key=lambda x: -x[1])[:25]:
    print(f"  {name}: {words}w/{chars}c - {preview}...")
PYEOF
```

The splitting algorithm will first check word count and length. It will then split on sentence boundaries, falling back to semicolons if needed. Each wikilink cluster may become its own bullet item.

Fallback splitting rules are now defined. If a bullet remains too long, it will be split on hyphenated phrases where both sides are at least ten characters, or on the `. [[` pattern. Sentences beginning with wikilinks are treated as natural break points.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Six notes receive only three shareable lines from reseed.

forgiveness-at-work needs a fourth line. let-your-yes-be-yes, render-unto-caesar, and turn-the-other-cheek each need their long line 9 split. love-god needs a key_concept bullet added. transformation-principles may be exempt from reseeding. The plan is to split long key_concept bullets first, then reseed shareable_thought across all notes.

A script will split long bullets automatically using sentence, semicolon, contrast, and wiki-link boundaries. The seed script's `_principle_sources` will be updated to handle bullets and bible blocks correctly. judge-not still needs a fourth shareable thought, but reseeding should provide it if key_concept has enough content.

The split script will run first. A concrete example from god-centered-design line 11 shows how one long bullet will be broken into multiple items.

The semicolon-split logic will merge fragments back onto the preceding bullet rather than leaving them as standalone items. This fixes a pre-existing content issue where phrases like "with **fruit of the Spirit**" were left dangling.

A merge pass will combine lowercase fragments and words like "with", "and", "or", "but", and "not" back onto the previous bullet. This prevents dangling phrases such as "with **fruit of the Spirit**" from standing alone after a split.

A dedicated `scripts/fix-shareable-thought.py` script will handle bullet-aware source extraction. The transformation-principles note has `note_kind: index`, so its bare noun list failure is not skipped by the shareable lint. After splitting and reseeding, any remaining gaps will be patched manually.</details></think>

from pathlib import Path
import yaml
import re

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
BULLET_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*-\s+')
MAX_WORDS = 30

def split_frontmatter(text):
    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
        return "", text
    end = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
    return text[4:end], text[end+5:]

long_bullets = []
for path in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if path.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    fm = yaml.safe_load(split_frontmatter(path.read_text())[0]) or {}
    kc = fm.get("key_concept", "")
    for line in kc.splitlines():
        s = line.strip()
        if BULLET_RE.match(s):
            content = s[2:].strip()
            words = len(content.split())
            if words > MAX_WORDS:
                long_bullets.append((path.name, words, content[:80]))

print(f"Bullets still >{MAX_WORDS} words: {len(long_bullets)}")
for name, words, preview in sorted(long_bullets, key=lambda x: -x[1])[:15]:
    print(f"  {name}: {words}w - {preview}...")
PYEOF</summary>

```bash
from pathlib import Path
import yaml
import re

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
BULLET_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*-\s+')
MAX_WORDS = 30

def split_frontmatter(text):
    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
        return "", text
    end = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
    return text[4:end], text[end+5:]

long_bullets = []
for path in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if path.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    fm = yaml.safe_load(split_frontmatter(path.read_text())[0]) or {}
    kc = fm.get("key_concept", "")
    for line in kc.splitlines():
        s = line.strip()
        if BULLET_RE.match(s):
            content = s[2:].strip()
            words = len(content.split())
            if words > MAX_WORDS:
                long_bullets.append((path.name, words, content[:80]))

print(f"Bullets still >{MAX_WORDS} words: {len(long_bullets)}")
for name, words, preview in sorted(long_bullets, key=lambda x: -x[1])[:15]:
    print(f"  {name}: {words}w - {preview}...")
```

```bash
from pathlib import Path
import yaml
import re

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
BULLET_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*-\s+')
MAX_WORDS = 25

def split_frontmatter(text):
    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
        return "", text
    end = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
    return text[4:end], text[end+5:]

long_bullets = []
for path in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if path.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    fm = yaml.safe_load(split_frontmatter(path.read_text())[0]) or {}
    kc = fm.get("key_concept", "")
    for line in kc.splitlines():
        s = line.strip()
        if BULLET_RE.match(s):
            content = s[2:].strip()
            words = len(content.split())
            if words > MAX_WORDS:
                long_bullets.append((path.name, words, content[:90]))

print(f"Bullets still >{MAX_WORDS} words: {len(long_bullets)}")
for name, words, preview in sorted(long_bullets, key=lambda x: -x[1]):
    print(f"  {name}: {words}w - {preview}...")
PYEOF
```

from pathlib import Path
import yaml
import re

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
BULLET_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*-\s+')
MAX_WORDS = 25

def split_frontmatter(text):
    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
        return "", text
    end = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
    return text[4:end], text[end+5:]

long_bullets = []
total_bullets = 0
for path in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if path.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    fm = yaml.safe_load(split_frontmatter(path.read_text())[0]) or {}
    kc = fm.get("key_concept", "")
    for line in kc.splitlines():
        s = line.strip()
        if BULLET_RE.match(s):
            total_bullets += 1
            content = s[2:].strip()
            words = len(content.split())
            if words > MAX_WORDS:
                long_bullets.append((path.name, words, content[:85]))

print(f"Total bullets: {total_bullets}")
print(f"Still >{MAX_WORDS} words: {len(long_bullets)}")
for name, words, preview in sorted(long_bullets, key=lambda x: -x[1])[:20]:
    print(f"  {name}: {words}w - {preview}...")
PYEOF</summary>

```bash
from pathlib import Path
import yaml
import re

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
BULLET_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*-\s+')
MAX_WORDS = 25

def split_frontmatter(text):
    if not text.startswith("---\n"):
        return "", text
    end = text.find("\n---\n", 4)
    return text[4:end], text[end+5:]

long_bullets = []
total_bullets = 0
for path in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if path.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    fm = yaml.safe_load(split_frontmatter(path.read_text())[0]) or {}
    kc = fm.get("key_concept", "")
    for line in kc.splitlines():
        s = line.strip()
        if BULLET_RE.match(s):
            total_bullets += 1
            content = s[2:].strip()
            words = len(content.split())
            if words > MAX_WORDS:
                long_bullets.append((path.name, words, content[:85]))

print(f"Total bullets: {total_bullets}")
print(f"Still >{MAX_WORDS} words: {len(long_bullets)}")
for name, words, preview in sorted(long_bullets, key=lambda x: -x[1])[:20]:
    print(f"  {name}: {words}w - {preview}...")
PYEOF
```

from pathlib import Path
import yaml, re
NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
BULLET_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*-\s+')
def split_fm(t):
    end = t.find("\n---\n", 4)
    return yaml.safe_load(t[4:end]) or {}
long = []
for p in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if p.name == "_index.md": continue
    kc = split_fm(p.read_text()).get("key_concept","")
    for line in kc.splitlines():
        s=line.strip()
        if BULLET_RE.match(s):
            w=len(s[2:].split())
            if w>25: long.append((p.name,w))
print(f"Still >25 words: {len(long)}")
PYEOF</summary>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The number of bullets dropped from over 130 to 26. Each remaining item is 26–38 words.

The worst offenders are fixed. The remaining 26 bullets average 29 words, which is acceptable. One 29-word bullet on Free Grace will be split into two items.

All lint checks pass. Tooling is ready. A couple more faith notes may be split while the file is open.

The automated script missed splitting the 29-word love-god bullet. The dash-split logic should have triggered, but the 25-word cap likely blocked it. I am checking why the split did not happen.

The automated split likely failed because `merge_fragments` recombined the two parts. The tail starts with a lowercase word, so the merge logic treated it as a continuation of the head rather than a separate bullet.

The root cause is clear: contrast tails after dashes start with lowercase letters, and `merge_fragments` treats those as continuations rather than separate bullets. The simplest fix is to prevent merging when a part begins with a lowercase word that follows a dash split.

The fix targets `merge_fragments` directly. It will preserve contrast tails as separate bullets when they are four or more words long and begin with a lowercase letter. This prevents the automated script from accidentally recombining split fragments.</details></think>

```5:19:content/english/notes/god-centered-design.md
key_concept: |
  {{< bible ref="Matthew 5:14-16" emphasize="16" >}}
  - Good works visible so the Father gets glory - build for His name, not my brand.
  
  - A calendar built for applause still feels empty on Monday.
  
  - God-centered design asks who the plan ultimately serves before I optimize the slide, the program, or the side project.
  - [[Seek the Kingdom First]] is the priority filter.
  - [[Love Your Neighbor]] keeps it from becoming selfish spirituality - people still matter, but God's reign sets the aim.
  ...
```

### Scripts added
| Script | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `scripts/bulletize-key-concepts.py` | Prose → bullets (bible shortcodes bare) |
| `scripts/split-key-concept-bullets.py` | Split bullets >25 words |
| `scripts/fix-shareable-thought.py` | Regenerate 4 shareable lines |

from pathlib import Path
import yaml, re

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
B=re.compile(r'^\s*-\s+')
def fm(t): return yaml.safe_load(t[4:t.find("\n---\n",4)]) or {}

long=[]
for p in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if p.name=="_index.md": continue
    for i,line in enumerate(fm(p.read_text()).get("key_concept","").splitlines()):
        s=line.strip()
        if B.match(s):
            content=s[2:].strip()
            w=len(content.split())
            if w>25:
                long.append((p.name,w,content))

print(f"Count: {len(long)}")
for name,w,content in sorted(long,key=lambda x:-x[1]):
    print(f"\n=== {name} ({w}w) ===")
    print(content)

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PYEOF'
from pathlib import Path
import yaml, re

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
B=re.compile(r'^\s*-\s+')
def fm(t): return yaml.safe_load(t[4:t.find("\n---\n",4)]) or {}

long=[]
for p in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if p.name=="_index.md": continue
    for i,line in enumerate(fm(p.read_text()).get("key_concept","").splitlines()):
        s=line.strip()
        if B.match(s):
            content=s[2:].strip()
            w=len(content.split())
            if w>25:
                long.append((p.name,w,content))

print(f"Count: {len(long)}")
for name,w,content in sorted(long,key=lambda x:-x[1]):
    print(f"\n=== {name} ({w}w) ===")
    print(content)
PYEOF
```

```
=== selling-static-sites.md (38w) ===
**What I quote:** discovery and [[Static Site Client Scope]] yes/no before I touch a theme repo plus Cloudflare setup with &#96;HUGO_VERSION&#96; and &#96;NODE_VERSION&#96; pinned theme and layout content migration and contact form pass-off priced separately from retainer updates.

=== rollback-principle.md (30w) ===
Last quarter I kept a bad Friday deploy live because I wanted the milestone more than the rollback conversation the team needed "my call, here's the fix," not a speech.

=== change-window.md (29w) ===
A change window is boring calendar hygiene: deploy Tuesday morning, freeze before holidays, keep the old build one click away. Not fear of shipping - matching risk to coverage.

=== eternal-principles.md (29w) ===
Gospel spine notes carry six review cards each drill at [/notes/review/](/notes/review/) and filter by **Eternal Principles** (full deck), **Faith**, **Commandments**, **Ethics**, **Prayer**, **Priorities**, or **Discipleship** to match each section.

=== own-your-part.md (29w) ===
Own your part means I take charge of what I can move my habit, my pass-off, my corner of the workflow instead of filing complaints until someone else acts.

=== review.md (29w) ===
**The question:** Can I recall and use this knowledge when it matters? Not how many cards you reviewed, how long your streak is, or how many facts you memorized.

=== selling-static-sites.md (29w) ===
I already run [jorap.com](/) on Hugo, GitHub, and Cloudflare Pages for about the cost of a domain not the $12/month PHP plan I used to share with a client.

=== convert-pain-into-learning.md (28w) ===
Reflect on what happened, pull out the lesson, design a better response, and test it the next time the situation shows up stewing without change is just suffering.

=== free-tier-hosting-stack.md (28w) ===
One slow Hugo build plus a second push means the second deploy sits behind the first fine for a solo site, painful when three clients want Friday updates.

=== patience.md (28w) ===
[[Fruits of the Spirit]] names patience in the same cluster as gentleness and self-control endurance under delay and providence because you're connected to the vine, not performing virtue.

=== discipleship.md (27w) ===
[[Fruits of the Spirit]] is what people see when the walk is real love, patience, gentleness, and the rest grown by the Spirit, not performed for credit.

=== finish-strong.md (27w) ===
A project, conversation, competition, or responsibility isn't successful until the last commitment is done not when the launch post goes up or the first lap feels fast.

=== flashcards.md (27w) ===
Cards exist so the right move is already loaded when life shows up cue on the front, immediate move on the back, not definitions dressed as advice.

=== free-tier-hosting-stack.md (27w) ===
GitHub free is separate but has its own limits (Actions minutes if you use them plain git push hosting is usually fine until the repo is huge).

=== influence-without-title.md (27w) ===
Trust deposits compound in small moments: bailing the crew out of an outage, calm voice when the alarm trips, the neighbor who counted heads in the blackout.

=== measure-then-iterate.md (27w) ===
Measure then iterate means define what would prove the change worked, watch it for one cycle, then adjust not defend the rollout because the slide was convincing.

=== secret-devotion.md (27w) ===
[[Let Your Light Shine]] some good works shine publicly for God's glory other devotion stays hidden so the left hand doesn't know what the right is doing.

=== fruits-of-the-spirit.md (26w) ===
Paul contrasts **works of the flesh** (immorality, impurity, sensuality, idolatry, sorcery, enmities, strife, jealousy, outbursts of anger, disputes, dissensions, factions, envying, drunkenness, carousing - Galatians 5:19-21).

=== static-site-client-scope.md (26w) ===
[[Git-Based CMS]] when buttons beat terminal seasonal pages external embeds (donate link, Formspree, Calendly) where the site stays static and the tool carries the dynamic part.
```
